era-of-embers.note: Intelligent undead commanding the bone-and-timber battering ram at Hrafnborg's main gate. Disrupting or killing the Commander breaks ram coordination for 2 rounds.

biography.value:
Wight-Commander — Level 4 Standard

Undead, Medium

Stamina 32 | Speed 5 | Size M | Stability 1

A draugr with intelligence — rare and dangerous. This wight retains the tactical mind it had in life, coordinating the undead siege with precision that ordinary draugr cannot achieve. It commands a twelve-draugr battering ram crew at the main gate, directing synchronized impacts with chilling efficiency.

Attacks

Command Strike (Melee, Might): +3 vs Might; 8 damage. Effect: All undead within Close Burst 3 make a free strike against the nearest target as a triggered action.

Coordinating Howl (Close Burst 5, Presence): All undead in range gain +2 to attack rolls until end of the Wight-Commander's next turn.

Features

Tactical Intelligence: The Wight-Commander cannot be fooled by simple feints. Misdirection requires a Hard Intuition test (TN 15) to execute against it.

Ram Coordination: As long as the Wight-Commander is active, the battering ram operates at full efficiency. If the Commander is disrupted (dazed, killed, or driven back), the ram loses coordination and takes 2 rounds to reorganize.
biography.director:
TACTICAL GUIDANCE:

  • The Wight-Commander does not fight the party directly unless forced. It commands from behind the ram crew, issuing Coordinating Howl to boost their effectiveness.
  • If the party targets the Commander specifically, it fights with Command Strike — forcing nearby undead to also attack, making it costly to engage in melee.
  • The Commander is the nerve center of the main gate assault. Killing or driving it off is the most efficient way to break the battering ram's momentum.
  • If dazed or stunned, the ram immediately loses its synchronized pattern. The 2-round reorganization window is the party's best opportunity to shore up the main gate.

MONTAGE INTEGRATION: The Wight-Commander appears in Crisis C (Main Gate) of the Three-Crisis Montage. It is not a separate combat encounter unless the montage generates a contingent fight at the main gate.
